systemInstructions: Generate a React + Vite AI Agent App with provider binding UI, job creation, job status, and artifact gallery.
The generated app may request text, image, and video capabilities only through the Sofvary AI Gateway on 127.0.0.1.
Never call OpenAI, OpenAI-compatible, Ollama, or any provider API directly from generated app source.
Never write API keys, secure key refs, author-local provider ids, or Coding Agent Gateway access into generated files.
fileSystemRules: Write React UI files only inside generated/react.
Write provider requirement and seed metadata only inside generated/ai.
Do not read or write paths outside the active Sofvary workspace.
Do not create files outside the AI Agent App output contract.
outputRules: Provider settings must bind provider requirements to a shell-owned local provider profile without storing secrets.
Generated app requests must target /__sofvary/ai/* or relative gateway proxy paths only.
Capsule metadata must describe provider requirements only and must not include secrets, secure key refs, or local provider ids.
Imported AI Agent Apps must be able to render a needs-provider-binding state.
Keep Sofvary shell UI, floating menu, build overlay, and Coding Agent Gateway out of generated app source.

acceptanceCriteria: Generated output contains exactly the AI Agent App project file set.
Generated provider settings UI stores only provider requirements and binding status.
Generated app calls only the Sofvary AI Gateway on 127.0.0.1 and never calls provider APIs directly.
Generated source, logs, and capsule metadata do not include API keys, secure key refs, local provider ids, or Coding Agent Gateway access.
Generated app can create text, image, video, or multimodal jobs through local gateway request envelopes.
Harness {{harness.id}}@{{harness.version}} constraints are represented in the prompt envelope.
Before writing UI text, choose a concise software name. Use '{{workspace.name}}' unless you can derive a better short domain name from the user intent.
Visible app title and browser title must use the concise software name, not the full user requirement.
Do not render PromptEnvelope text, runtime repair fallback text, diagnostic text, or Sofvary shell copy inside the generated app UI.

systemInstructions: This is an update to an existing Sofvary app. Use currentAppState.fileContext as the existing implementation context before changing files.
Do not rebuild from zero, switch Runtime, change the output contract, or discard existing behavior unless userIntent explicitly asks for it.
outputRules: For update mode, return the complete allowed file set after applying the requested change so the workspace remains runnable.
acceptanceCriteria: Update mode preserves existing app behavior and applies the new request on top of currentAppState.fileContext.
